rulings.Case Title: TONY GARNICKI ET AL VS. ANCHOR LOANS LP, ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 22, 2022 line 2. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED, in part, and OVERRULED, in part. Special Demurrer: Defendants demurrer on the grounds of Capacity to Sue, Tender, and Uncertainty is overruled. With respect to Lack of Standing as to Garnicki and Ocean LLC, the demurrer is overruled; the demurrer as to Sanchez RE is sustained with leave to amend. (Bostanian v. Liberty Savings Bank (1997) 52 Cal.App.4th 1075, 1084.) A cause of action accruing after a bankruptcy petition is filed is property of the bankruptcy estate. The bankruptcy estate is comprised of property including: all legal or equitable interests of the debtor in property as of the commencement of the case (11 United States Code Sec. 541(a)(1)), and [a]ny interest in property that the estate acquires after the commencement of the case. (11 United States Code Sec. 541(a)(7).) [P]roperty includes causes of action. (United States v.Whiting Pools, Inc. (1983) 462 U.S. 198, 205, fn. 9.) Here, Sanchez REs claims accrued on April 21, 2020 when the foreclosure sales occurred on the Corbett and Mississippi propertieswhich was after the February 17, 2020 filing of the Chapter 7 petition. Accordingly, their claims are property of the bankruptcy estate. (11 United States Code Sec. 541(a)(7).) Additionally, a cause of action relating to an asset of the estate is also property of the bankruptcy estate. Property of the bankruptcy estate includes: Proceeds, product, offspring, rents, or profits of or from property of the estate.... (11 United States Code Sec. 541(a)(6).) Here, Sanchez RE had a legal or equitable interest in the Corbett and Mississippi properties at the time of the bankruptcy filing, and upon commencement of the bankruptcy case, Sanchez REs interest in the real property became vested in the estate. (11 United States Code Sec. 541(a)(1).) A claim for improper foreclosure is a product, proceed, or offspring of Sanchez REs interest in the two properties, and therefore also property of the estate under Section 541(a)(6). Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend to secure an abandonment of the claims by the trustee in the bankruptcy court. General Demurrer: 1st and 2nd causes of action: The demurrer is overruled. Defendants re-argue the same arguments from their special demurrer, namely, capacity to sue, uncertainty, and lack of standing. 3rd cause of action: The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Injunctive relief is a remedy and not a cause of action. (City of South Pasadena v. Department of Transportation (1994) 29 Cal.App.4th 1280, 1293.) Furthermore, the request for injunctive relief is moot. Foreclosure sales have already taken place on all four properties and Garnicki has been dispossessed from the Corbett property, his prior residence. 4th cause of action: The demurrer is overruled. Anchors request for judicial notice is denied. Anchors search result printouts from a website purporting to show they are CFL licensed is not the proper subject of judicial notice under Evid. Code Sec. 452(g) or (h) (common knowledge or verifiable facts not reasonably subject to dispute). 5th cause of action: The demurrer is overruled. The SAC sufficiently alleges irregularities in the proceedings. (See, e.g., SAC, ¶¶ 32, 34, 36, 44, 45 47, 53, 54, 56, 62,63, 65, 66.) And Plaintiffs invoke all four exceptions to the tender requirement. (SAC, ¶ 150.)

rulings.Case Title: OASIS GRILL, LLC ET AL VS. ERIC KETCHUM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION That Oasis' Green Tag Breach Of Contract Cause Of Action Has No Merit
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 22, 2022 line 3. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUCICATION THAT OASIS' GREEN TAG BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION HAS NO MERIT is DENIED. The facts put forth by Defendant do not establish that Plaintiff cannot show causation and damages resulting from the alleged usurpation of the green tag. Therefore, Defendant does not shift the burden to Plaintiff to show there is a triable issue of fact. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: OASIS GRILL, LLC ET AL VS. ERIC KETCHUM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION That Oasis' "Failure To Maintain" Breach Of Contract Cause Of Action Is Time-Barred
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 22, 2022 line 3. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION THAT OASIS' "FAILURE TO MAINTAIN" BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION IS TIME-BARRED is DENIED. Defendant does not put forth facts that show that all elements of the breach of contract cause of action accrued when the lease was signed in 2015, such as Plaintiff's damages resulting from the breach. Oasis West Realty, LLC v. Goldman (2011) 51 Cal.4th 811, 821. Therefore, Defendant does not make a prima facie showing that the cause of action is time-barred, and Defendant does not shift the burden to Plaintiff to show there is a triable issue of fact. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
